title: XP, The Charles Schwab Of Brazil, Nears Early Entry As Bovespa Heats Up

title: 3 Battered Blue-Chip Stocks to Sell in December
text: The market continues to rip higher in November, with the S&P 500 up 9% since Halloween. However, not all stocks are participating in the rally.

title: Charles Schwab Is A Buy At This Price
text: Charles Schwab's revenues have increased significantly over the last decade, with a 266% increase, and its EPS has increased by 285%. The company is a reliable dividend payer and offers a 1.8% yield, with the potential for high-single digit dividend growth. The successful integration of TD Ameritrade presents a significant growth opportunity for Charles Schwab, along with growing demand for wealth management services and the Workplace business.

title: 5 Struggling Stocks to Buy at a Discount
text: These five companies have glimmers of hope that the market has yet to fully recognize. Each is trading at a significant discount to recent highs.

title: Charles Schwab: Expecting A Bottom At The Technical Support Level
text: Charles Schwab Corporation's Q3 2023 earnings reflect financial challenges, but the company remains resilient and adaptable in a volatile economic and political environment. Despite a decrease in net income, Schwab's long-term trend remains positive. Technical analysis suggests that Schwab's stock price is consolidating at critical levels, indicating a possible base formation and potential for future growth.

title: Charles Schwab: Buy, Sell, or Hold?
text: Charles Schwab has dealt with declining deposits for over a year as the Federal Reserve engaged in its most aggressive interest rate hiking campaign in 40 years. The company's deposit declines have slowed materially in the past several months, and it continues to attract new client assets.

title: Off 42% From Highs, What's Wrong With Charles Schwab Stock?
text: Charles Schwab has done a great job growing its customers and integrating its TD Ameritrade merger. The company made some mistakes with low-interest-rate loans it purchased and is facing a headwind from higher interest rates.

title: My Top 15 High Growth Dividend Stocks For November 2023
text: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF posted its third consecutive losing month in October, the first time this has happened since I started tracking this series in September 2020. The top 15 dividend growth stocks for November offer an average dividend yield of 1.90% and appear to be about 40% undervalued based on dividend yield theory. Since inception, September 2020, the watchlist is outperforming VIG by 1.49% and SPY by 1.18%, on an annualized basis.

title: Charles Schwab Layoffs 2023: What to Know About the Latest SCHW Job Cuts
text: Brokerage and wealth management advisory service provider Charles Schwab (NYSE: SCHW ) certainly hasn't been a darling of the financial market this year. Now, a recent declaration of Charles Schwab layoffs may make some investors more anxious about the company — and even about the nation's economy.

title: Charles Schwab cutting as many as 2,000 jobs — joining Goldman, Morgan Stanley
text: "These were hard but necessary steps to ensure Schwab remains highly competitive," the spokesperson said in an emailed statement.

title: Schwab reportedly laid off up to 2,000 employees; analyst reiterates outperform
text: Charles Schwab Corp.'s stock SCHW fell 0.5% on Wednesday on the heels of a report by RIABiz that the broker has laid off up to 2,000 employees this week as part of a previously disclosed effort to save $500 million in the second half of the year. William Blair analyst Jeff Schmitt reiterated an outperform rating on Schwab “due to the potential for a significant rebound in EPS in 2024 and 2025 as cash sorting abates, short-term funding costs decline, client cash stabilizes, organic growth returns to historic levels and share buybacks reemerge,” Schmitt said in a research note on Tuesday.
